Impeccably dressed in a business suit.

A wrist brace was visible on her arm.

“Miss Tremblay?” Sophia feigned surprise. “What are you doing here?”

“A check-up,” Zoe explained, gesturing to her wrist. “An old injury. It acts up sometimes. Especially after long hours of drawing.”

###Chapter 427

Zoe studied Sophia with a thoughtful gaze. "I'm surprised you returned from Salzburg so quickly. Mr. Blackwood must still value you highly."

Sophia's expression tightened before she forced a polite smile. "You flatter me, Miss Tremblay."

Zoe raised an elegant eyebrow. "Let's have coffee. There's something I'd like to discuss."

After a brief hesitation, Sophia nodded in agreement.

They selected a window table at a nearby upscale café.

"Two lattes, please," Zoe told the waiter before turning back to Sophia. "Since you left the city, Isabella has become even more insufferable."

Sophia felt her stomach clench. "What has she done now?"

Zoe gave a light, mocking laugh. "Everyone in elite circles sees her as Ethan's perfect match. Her influence keeps growing. People fear her. Former chief designer at Apex Design Collective, now Mrs. Blackwood... she's become quite powerful."

Sophia's fingers tightened around her newly arrived coffee cup, her knuckles pressing white against the porcelain. "What gives her the right to act so superior?"

"Exactly." Zoe took a delicate sip of her coffee before setting it down. "Men always chase after new excitement. Once the novelty wears off, Ethan will come back to you."

Sophia hesitated, biting her lip. "But Ethan seems genuinely devoted to her."

"Devotion? That's just an act." Zoe scoffed dismissively. "Do you actually believe men are capable of true devotion? Look what changing yourself for him has gotten you."

Each word struck Sophia like a physical blow, leaving her sitting in painful silence.

"Will you just stand by while Isabella takes everything?" Zoe's tone turned coaxing. "Are you willing to lose to her?"

A fierce fire ignited in Sophia's eyes as she lifted her head sharply. "No. I won't let that happen. Ethan belongs to me."

"Precisely," Zoe responded with an approving nod. "We share a common enemy. Perhaps we should join forces again."

Sophia stared at Zoe, conflicting emotions flickering across her face.

"What kind of collaboration did you have in mind?"

"Are you familiar with the International Jewelry Design Competition?" Zoe asked.

Sophia remained silent, waiting for her to continue, her grip tightening slightly on her coffee cup.

"There's potential for her to shine there... or for us to intervene," Zoe said, her eyes sparkling with mischief.

Sophia frowned, perplexed. "What exactly are you suggesting?"

Zoe leaned closer, lowering her voice. "Imagine if something unexpected happened during the competition. Consider the implications."

A chill ran through Sophia as she envisioned scenarios of Isabella's potential downfall.

"You mean to suggest..."

Zoe raised her hands defensively, feigning innocence. "I'm merely pointing out that accidents can happen, right?"

Sophia's grip on her coffee cup tightened until her knuckles turned white, her mind racing with possibilities.

Would Ethan stay by Isabella's side if her reputation was destroyed during the competition?

The answer seemed obvious.

"I appreciate your advice, Miss Tremblay." Sophia exhaled sharply. "I've decided what to do next."

"You're welcome," Zoe responded, sipping her coffee with a subtle smile playing on her lips.

###Chapter 428

Isabella stood up.
Her body was tense, but her steps were firm and resolute.

Zoe watched her go from her seat.
A sly, satisfied smile played on her lips as she sipped her coffee.
Her hands would remain clean this time.

She had masterfully turned Isabella into a weapon against their shared rival, Sophia.
If Isabella succeeded, it was a bonus.
If she failed, Zoe lost nothing.
She had orchestrated everything from the shadows, simply nudging Isabella in the right direction.

She placed her cup down.
Her smile grew wider.

In the downtown hospital.

A soft knock broke the quiet of Eleanor’s room.

Isabella stirred, blinking as her eyes adjusted to the low light.
“Come in.”

The door opened smoothly.
The Blackwood family butler entered, carrying a thermos with a warm smile.
“Miss Scott, Mr. Blackwood asked our chef to prepare this broth. He insisted it would aid your recovery.”

He set the thermos on the bedside table.
A rich, savory aroma immediately filled the space.

Isabella looked at the soup, her emotions conflicted.
She felt gratitude, but also a deep unease.
“Please thank Mr. Blackwood for me,” she said, offering a polite but distant smile.

“Of course, Miss Scott. Mr. Blackwood also wished me to remind you that if you or Mrs. Vance require anything at all, we are at your service,” the butler replied.
He opened the container and poured a bowl of the steaming broth for her.

Isabella eyed it warily.
Ethan’s persistent attention made her uncomfortable.
She did not want to be in his debt.
“Really, that’s unnecessary. I am feeling much better now.”

“Please, Miss Scott. It is a gesture of goodwill from Mr. Blackwood,” the butler urged kindly. “And Mrs. Vance could benefit from the nourishment as well.”

Finally, Isabella relented under his earnest persuasion.
“Thank you.”

He smiled and left the room.

She held the warm bowl but couldn't bring herself to eat.
Ethan’s concern felt overwhelming, a reminder of the complicated ties binding her, Liam, and him.

A soft cough came from the bed.

Isabella’s gaze snapped to her grandmother.
Eleanor’s eyelids fluttered, a sign she was waking.

“Grandmother!” Isabella cried, abandoning the bowl to grasp her grandmother’s hand. “You’re awake!”

Eleanor’s eyes opened slowly, her gaze hazy and unfocused.
“Is that you, Isabella?”

“Yes, Grandmother. It’s me. It’s really me,” Isabella responded, her voice choked with emotion as tears streamed down her face.

Seeing Eleanor conscious washed away a wave of her earlier anxiety.
She immediately pressed the call button by the bed.

A doctor arrived shortly to conduct a thorough examination.

###Chapter 429

“She is stable for now, but her condition remains fragile. Complete rest is essential. Any further stress could lead to severe complications,” the doctor stated with a serious tone.

“Thank you, Doctor,” Isabella replied gratefully.

After seeing the doctor out, Isabella returned to Eleanor’s bedside. She watched her grandmother with deep concern.

“How are you feeling, Grandma? Is there any pain?”

Eleanor looked at Isabella with loving yet troubled eyes.

“Isabella, you must be honest with me. Did you truly come between another woman and her relationship?”

The question took Isabella by surprise.

She was confused by Eleanor’s sudden inquiry.

“Grandma, what are you talking about? I would never do something like that…”

“But that woman told me you did,” Eleanor whispered, her voice trembling slightly.

A cold dread washed over Isabella. She knew Eleanor was referring to Sophia.

Eleanor’s memory of that confrontation was painfully clear.

“She accused you of being the other woman. She said you destroyed her relationship. And Liam… who is his father?”

Isabella lowered her head, remaining silent.

She did not want to burden Eleanor with those painful details. Yet the worry in her grandmother’s eyes demanded an answer.

“Grandma,” Isabella began, taking a steadying breath. “I never interfered in anyone’s relationship. Liam is Ethan’s son.”

Her voice was soft but firm.

Eleanor’s expression filled with sorrow as she gazed at Isabella.

“My dear child, you have carried such a heavy burden alone.”

“Grandma…” Isabella’s voice broke. Tears welled in her eyes as she squeezed Eleanor’s hand. “Please, you must keep this a secret. Don’t tell anyone who Liam’s father is.”

Eleanor’s eyes were full of emotion.

“I promise, my dear.”

Meanwhile, in the CEO’s office atop Blackwood Enterprises headquarters, a knock interrupted Ethan’s concentration.

“Come in,” Ethan commanded.

Benjamin entered respectfully and approached the desk.

“Mr. Blackwood, Mrs. Vance’s condition has stabilized. She is out of immediate danger.”

A slight relief softened Ethan’s features.

“Additionally, your grandfather has arranged a meeting with your wife tomorrow at ten a.m.,” Benjamin continued.

“Understood,” Ethan replied, his voice even though a shadow of concern flickered in his eyes. “Ensure the meeting proceeds without any interruptions.”

“Yes, Mr. Blackwood.”

With that, Benjamin left the office.

Ethan turned his gaze to the darkening city skyline outside.

Suddenly, the phone on his desk rang sharply.

He pressed the speaker button.

“Mr. Blackwood, Miss Reed is here. She is demanding to see you immediately,” the receptionist’s tense voice announced.

###Chapter 430


Ethan’s expression tightened with clear annoyance.

“I’m busy. Tell her to go.”

“But Miss Reed insists on seeing you. She refuses to leave,” the receptionist replied, her voice strained.

Ethan pinched the bridge of his nose, a dull ache forming behind his eyes.

“Fine. Let her come up.”

Minutes later, the door opened.

Sophia entered with practiced grace.

She wore a simple white dress, her hair loose around her shoulders. Her makeup was light, almost natural.

“Ethan,” she said softly, her eyes pleading.

Her bottom lip trembled slightly. Tears glistened at the corners of her eyes.

“I need you to know—I never intended to hurt Isabella’s grandmother. What happened was—”

“I don’t care. Stop explaining,” Ethan cut in, his tone sharp.

Sophia tried to speak again, but his cold stare silenced her.

“If that’s all, you should leave,” he stated firmly.

She took a slow breath, steadying herself.

“My birthday is in a few days. I was hoping we could spend it together. Like we used to.”

Ethan was quiet for a moment.

Then he gave a short nod.

“Alright.”

A flicker of hope lit up Sophia’s face.

“Thank you, Ethan.”

“Anything else?” he asked, his attention already drifting.

She hesitated.

“Your mother… she spoke out of turn that day. Please don’t be angry with her. She only wants what’s best for you.”

Ethan didn’t respond. He looked down at the papers on his desk, effectively ending the conversation.

Understanding the dismissal, Sophia turned and left quietly.

The office fell into heavy silence.

Ethan stared at the documents, but the words blurred together.

His mind was elsewhere.

He kept seeing Isabella’s sharp, determined eyes.

That evening, Ethan arrived at Hawthorne Residence.

He loosened his tie as he stepped inside.

The house felt too quiet. Too empty.

It was never this still when Isabella was home.

He headed straight for his study, intent on burying himself in work.

“Mr. Blackwood?”

A small voice stopped him.

Ethan turned.

Liam sat on the carpet, playing quietly with a set of toy cars.

The boy’s wide eyes were full of confusion.

“Did my mommy leave me?” he whispered, his voice trembling slightly.

###Chapter 431
Ethan froze, caught completely by surprise.

He moved across the room and knelt beside the small boy.

Liam was in cartoon-patterned pajamas, his hair tousled and cheeks damp with tears. He looked tiny and fragile.

Ethan had never seen him like this before.

Isabella always made sure their son was cheerful and well-cared for.

Liam sniffled quietly.

“Henry said Mommy had work and told me to be good and go to sleep. But… I just can’t.”

Ethan reached out, gently stroking the boy’s soft hair.

“She’ll be home soon,” he reassured him.

Liam looked up, his eyes wide with cautious hope.

“Really?”

“Really,” Ethan confirmed with a firm nod.

He picked up the red toy Ferrari from Liam’s hand.

“This is your favorite, isn’t it?”

Liam nodded, a tiny spark returning to his gaze.

Ethan set the car on the floor and gave it a gentle push. It glided smoothly across the carpet.

“Look how fast it goes.”

Liam’s attention shifted entirely to the toy. A small grin appeared as he watched the car roll.

For a while, Ethan sat with him, playing along. Soon, the boy was fully absorbed in his toys, no longer mentioning Isabella.

As Ethan watched him, a complex wave of emotions washed over him.

Outside, the night pressed darkly against the windows.

Isabella sat beside Eleanor’s bed, her eyes fixed on her grandmother’s peacefully sleeping face.

Eleanor’s breathing was slow and steady.

Isabella let out a quiet sigh, though the heaviness in her chest remained.

She had no intention of returning to Hawthorne Residence—a place that felt suffocating, filled with nothing but pain.

All she wanted was to take Eleanor and Liam far away from it all. Somewhere they could live a simple, quiet life together.

The small apartment she rented was modest, but it was a shelter. A safe place.

There, she wouldn’t have to navigate the endless conflicts with the Blackwood family or defend against Sophia’s schemes. It would just be her, Eleanor, and Liam—taking care of each other.

The thought brought a fragile glimmer of hope to her heart.

She picked up her phone and dialed Benjamin’s number.

The ringing tone echoed in the quiet room, making her pulse quicken.

“Benjamin, it’s me.” Her voice was soft, carrying a slight tremor.

“Mrs. Blackwood, good evening,” came the polite reply.

“I don’t want to go back to Hawthorne Residence anymore.” Isabella took a steadying breath, forcing the words out.

A pause stretched between them before Benjamin responded, his tone uncertain.

“Pardon?”

“I mean, I want to bring my grandmother and Liam to stay with me at the place I’m renting,” Isabella replied, her voice firm with resolve.
###Chapter 432
“Well, this is…” Benjamin hesitated, clearly uneasy. “Mrs. Blackwood, I don’t have the authority to decide that. Mr. Blackwood’s consent is necessary.”
A cold dread settled deep in Isabella's chest, a heavy weight that made it hard to breathe.
She truly had no other options left.
"I understand," she whispered, the words barely audible, thick with a disappointment she could no longer hide.
"Mrs. Blackwood—" Benjamin started, but Isabella cut him off sharply.
"Enough," she stated, her tone final, ending the discussion.
She leaned back in her chair, closing her eyes. Silent tears traced paths down her cheeks.
The once beautiful Hawthorne Residence now felt like a luxurious cage, a prison she desperately needed to escape.
She had to stop hoping.
Ethan's heart wasn't hers to keep. Clinging to a hollow marriage was pointless.
Leaving with Eleanor and Liam to build their own life, away from this suffocating atmosphere, was what she truly desired.
Benjamin stared at his phone, the screen now dark. His brows were furrowed with concern. The tone of Isabella's voice had left him deeply unsettled; he understood the gravity of the situation completely.
Without delay, he composed a message to Ethan, relaying the news.
"Mr. Blackwood, Mrs. Blackwood just called. She stated she has no intention of returning to Hawthorne Residence. She plans to move into her rented apartment with her grandmother and son."
He hit send, a knot of unease tightening in his stomach. He knew Ethan's temperament, especially concerning Isabella.
He sighed quietly, hoping—praying—that Ethan would recognize Isabella's pain and not let the situation deteriorate further.
In his dimly lit study, Ethan was buried in paperwork, his expression severe, his focus intense.
Then his phone buzzed. He glanced at the screen and saw Benjamin's message.
As he read the words, his pupils constricted sharply. The documents in his hand slipped from his grasp, scattering across the floor with a sharp, rustling sound.
"She refuses to come back?" he muttered under his breath, disbelief coloring his low tone.
He shot to his feet, pacing the room in agitation. Flashes of Isabella's face tormented his mind.
"Damn it!" His fist slammed onto the solid wood of his desk, the sound echoing loudly in the quiet room.
He grabbed his phone, his thumb hovering over Isabella's number—but he hesitated.
Instead, he sank back into his leather chair, dragging a hand down his face. He exhaled a long, weary breath.
What was he supposed to do now?
Was he really going to lose her?
Inside his study, Ethan tossed his phone onto the desk in sheer irritation. The screen still displayed Benjamin's message.
Isabella wanted to leave. She wanted to take Eleanor and Liam with her. She wanted to vanish from his life for good.
He tugged at his tie, a frustrated huff escaping his lips. He walked to the expansive floor-to-ceiling window that offered a panoramic view of the glittering city below.
The lights from the buildings and streets looked bright and inviting, but to Ethan, they only emphasized his profound isolation.
"How," he muttered, the disbelief clear in his voice, "how can she just leave like this?"
His mind filled with images of Isabella's gentle smile and the sound of Liam's innocent laughter. A dull, persistent ache began to spread through his chest.
He couldn't lose them. He wouldn't.
The thought of Liam suddenly gave Ethan an idea. Perhaps he should start with the little boy.
Ethan strode back to his desk and snatched up his phone to call Henry.
###Chapter 433

“Good morning, Mr. Blackwood,” Henry greeted with deference.

“Is Liam still sleeping?”

“Yes, Mr. Blackwood.”

“Very well. I need you to go to the hospital tomorrow.” Ethan’s voice was firm and left no room for argument.

After ending the call, his expression hardened with resolve.

If Isabella wanted to remain with him, she would have no choice but to stay as well.

Morning light poured through the hospital window, illuminating the sterile room.

The door opened quietly. A well-dressed middle-aged man entered. It was Henry.

Isabella looked up, startled. The atmosphere instantly grew thick with tension. Her nerves felt like they were pulled taut.

She rose to her feet, her tone polite but guarded.

“Did Mr. Blackwood send you with a message?”

Henry offered a slight, respectful bow.

“Mr. Blackwood has requested your presence at Blackwood Enterprises. Liam misses you, Mrs. Blackwood.”

“Blackwood Enterprises?” Isabella repeated, her brow furrowing in confusion.

“Yes,” Henry confirmed. “Mr. Blackwood took Liam to the office.”

A cold dread settled in Isabella’s stomach.

Ethan had taken their son to the company?

What was that man plotting now?

Her lips pressed into a thin, worried line. Her mind raced.

She had no desire to see Ethan. That hadn't changed. But his sudden interest in their son filled her with a confusing mix of fear and suspicion.

She couldn't stop wondering what his game was.

She took a steadying breath and met Henry’s gaze.

“Mrs. Blackwood,” Henry prompted, his tone still respectful.

Isabella glanced back at her grandmother one last time. She finally gave a reluctant nod.

Given the circumstances, she had to see Ethan. She needed to understand his intentions, whether she wanted to or not.

She leaned close to her grandmother’s bedside.

“I’ll be back soon, Grandma.”

Then she followed Henry out of the room.

Less than an hour later, the elevator doors opened onto the top floor of Blackwood Enterprises. Isabella followed Henry into a stunning reception area outside the CEO's office.

The panoramic view of the city stretched behind towering glass walls. The décor was minimalist yet exuded luxury. It felt more like a high-end hotel lobby.

“Please wait here a moment, Mrs. Blackwood,” Henry said before knocking on the heavy office door.

“Enter,” a deep, familiar voice commanded from within.

Henry opened the door and stepped aside, gesturing for her to go in.

She stepped into the office—and froze.

All the furniture in Ethan’s vast office had been pushed against the walls. In the center of the plush carpet was a massive, chaotic mountain of toys. Cars, robots, building blocks, and stuffed animals were scattered everywhere.
﻿
###Chapter 434

Liam was right in the center of it all.

He giggled uncontrollably, a bright red Transformer clutched in his small hands. The sound of his laughter filled the spacious room.

Ethan sat next to him on the plush carpet. He held a sleek, black remote-controlled car. He was showing Liam how to operate the controller, his movements patient and deliberate.

The usual sternness was gone from his face. It was replaced by a soft, almost tender expression.

Isabella stood frozen in the doorway. She watched the scene unfold, her mind struggling to process it.

“Mommy!” Liam’s sharp eyes found her immediately. He dropped the toy and ran to her, his little feet pattering across the floor.

She bent down and gathered him into her arms. She held him tightly against her chest.

“Look, Mommy! So many new toys!” He pointed a chubby finger at the enormous pile of gifts. His entire face glowed with pure joy.

Her gaze lifted, finding Ethan’s. Her expression was a turbulent mix of confusion and dread.

“What is the meaning of this, Ethan?”

He rose to his feet and walked toward her. A faint, knowing smile played on his lips.

“Isabella,” he said, his voice deceptively soft. “Can’t you see how happy Liam is here? Doesn’t that matter to you?” He watched her carefully, gauging her reaction.

A cold dread settled in the pit of her stomach. She understood his game perfectly.

She looked down at her son’s eager, upturned face. “Liam, sweetheart, do you like being here?”

He nodded vigorously, his eyes wide. “I love it! There are cars and robots and everything!”

A sharp, aching pain lanced through her heart.

“We can’t stay somewhere just because of toys, baby,” she said, struggling to keep her voice even.

“Why not?” Liam asked, his small brow furrowing in genuine confusion.

Ethan seized the opportunity. He stepped closer.

“Liam,” he said, his tone gentle but firm. “All of these toys can be yours. Forever. But only if you and your mom decide to stay. If you leave, they have to stay here too.”

Liam’s eyes went round. “All of them? For me?”

“Every single one,” Ethan confirmed with a nod.

Liam turned back to Isabella, his expression full of hopeful pleading. “Please, Mommy? Can we stay? Please?”

The ache in Isabella’s chest intensified. She was trapped by her son’s innocent desire. She saw Ethan’s manipulation clearly, but denying Liam felt impossible.

“Sweetie, toys break. They get old. We can’t make big decisions based on things that won’t last,” she explained as gently as she could.

“Isabella.” Ethan’s voice cut through her explanation. “I only want to provide stability for him. A home where he can be safe and happy. Isn’t that what you want, too?”

She took a deep, steadying breath. Gently, she set Liam back down on his feet.

She met Ethan’s gaze directly, her voice laced with defiance. “What is your real goal here, Ethan? Just say it.”

Ethan didn’t answer immediately. Instead, he knelt down so he was eye-level with Liam. He spoke with a gentle sincerity that felt dangerous.

“Liam,” he said softly. “Tell your mom what you really want. Do you want to stay here with me?”

Liam’s eyes darted between his mother’s tense face and the magnificent mountain of colorful toys. He hesitated for only a second before giving a small, hopeful nod.

“I want to stay.”

Isabella felt her world tilt. Her heart plummeted.

###Chapter 435

The pure happiness shining in Liam's eyes twisted Isabella's heart into a painful knot.

It was the simple joy a child finds in new toys.

Liam had never known his biological father from the moment he was born.

Now that he finally had the chance, Isabella couldn't bear to take that away.

Even if Liam didn't yet realize Ethan was that man.

Isabella clenched her fists so tightly her nails nearly broke the skin of her palms.

She was determined to get Eleanor out of Ethan's house immediately.

Staying any longer risked further harm.

To Isabella, Ethan's home was nothing but a gilded cage.

Beautiful and inviting on the surface, yet ultimately suffocating.

Ethan watched her internal struggle, his brow furrowed with concern.

"Isabella, please. Let's discuss this."

"Discuss what?" Her voice cracked, tears threatening to fall.

"Do you think you can buy my compromise with toys? That I'll just forget everything that's happened?"

"No, let me explain," Ethan said urgently, his tone sincere. "You misunderstood me three years ago. I never intended to hurt you."

"Misunderstood you?" Tears streamed down Isabella's cheeks.

"And what about what happened to my grandmother recently? Was that another misunderstanding?"

Her voice trembled with emotion, barely containing her sobs.

"Ethan, you owe me. You owe my grandmother. How do you plan to make this right?"

The sight of her tears pained him deeply.

He reached out instinctively to comfort her, but Isabella slapped his hand away.

"Don't touch me!" she snapped, stepping back with a fierce glare.

She turned resolutely to her son.

"Liam, we're leaving."

Liam hesitated, his eyes lingering on the pile of toys.

"But Mom, the toys…"

Isabella crouched down to meet Liam's gaze. She fought back tears as she spoke.

"These toys are wonderful, sweetheart, but we can't sacrifice our principles for them. We need our own home. We can't keep depending on others."

"But I like it here…" Liam whined, his lips forming a pout.

Isabella's heart ached seeing him like this, but she refused to yield.

"I promise you, okay? I'll buy you so many toys in the future. Even better ones than these."

With that, she picked up Liam and carried him toward the door.

"Wait, Isabella!" Ethan called out desperately after them.

But Isabella ignored him and continued walking.

The office door slammed shut behind her, making Benjamin flinch.

Standing beside the door, he glanced at the scattered toys.

Ethan had each toy specially airlifted from overseas, every one as expensive as the transportation costs.

Liam had been playing so happily just moments ago, and now…

###Chapter 436
Ethan took a careful step toward the desk.
His voice was low and quiet.
“Mr. Blackwood…”

He leaned back in his leather chair.
His fingers were tented in front of him.
His gaze remained fixed on the closed door.

His expression made Benjamin pause.
“The toys…” he tried again, his words barely audible.

“Send them back to Hawthorne Residence,” Ethan said.
His tone was low and weary.

“Yes, Mr. Blackwood.” Benjamin didn’t ask anything else.
He quickly knelt and began gathering the toys one by one.

The office was unnervingly silent.
Only the soft rustle of toys being collected broke the quiet.

Ethan never looked away from the door.
His mind replayed Isabella walking out—her straight posture, her determined steps.

He thought he could fix things.
He was clearly mistaken.

Ethan’s hands clenched into tight fists.
His knuckles turned white.
Veins bulged on the back of his hands.

“Benjamin,” he said after a long moment, his voice rough.

“Yes, Mr. Blackwood?” Benjamin stood and brushed off his trousers.

“Which kindergarten does Liam attend?”

Benjamin looked surprised.
He hadn’t expected that question.

He thought for a moment before answering carefully.
“Liam attended one overseas. Since coming back to Switzerland, he goes to Sunny Meadows Kindergarten.”

Ethan’s brows furrowed.

“Actually, Mr. Blackwood… well, I heard from Henry that next Monday is Parents’ Day at Sunny Meadows. Parents take their children and spend the day there.” Benjamin spoke cautiously. Given Ethan’s mood, he knew to be careful.

Ethan tapped his fingers on the desk.
Each dull thud seemed to echo in Benjamin’s own chest.

The tension in the air grew thick.

“Cancel all my appointments for next Monday,” Ethan finally said.
His voice was filled with new resolve.

“All of them?” Benjamin repeated.

In response, Ethan turned and gave him a sharp look.

“Understood, Mr. Blackwood.” Benjamin nodded quickly and lowered his head.
He silently repeated the word all in his mind.

He finished packing the toys swiftly.
Then he exited the office quietly.

The moment he stepped into the reception area, he let out a long, heavy breath.
It felt like a weight was lifted from his shoulders.

But then a thought occurred to him.
There was an issue.

Benjamin hesitated.
He debated with himself for a moment.
Then he knocked on the office door again and entered.

“Excuse me, Mr. Blackwood…”

“What is it now?” Ethan asked impatiently.

“Next Monday is also Miss Reed’s birthday,” Benjamin said.
Then he held his breath, waiting for the reaction.

A heavy silence filled the room.
It was thick with unspoken tension.

###Chapter 437
Benjamin’s heart hammered violently against his ribs.
He was terrified it might burst right out of his chest.
“I know,” Ethan finally stated, his tone icy and detached.
Benjamin stood frozen. Mr. Blackwood had known—and yet he still commanded everything be called off?
“Get out,” Ethan ordered sharply.
Without uttering another word, Benjamin turned and left the room.
Outside, he stopped, drawing a deep breath to compose himself before resuming his duties.

The apartment smelled faintly of dust and disuse.
Isabella dropped her suitcase, inhaled deeply, and tried to force down the bitterness welling inside her.
Thankfully, Liam was instantly captivated by the cartoon playing on television.
Isabella took a moment to call Emily, the nanny who had cared for Liam before.
She gave a brief explanation of her situation, and Emily agreed to come over immediately.

Isabella glanced at Liam, now completely absorbed in the show, and started cleaning the apartment.
She dusted surfaces and unpacked her suitcase, sorting her belongings with methodical precision.
Her motions were robotic, her mind blank.
While wiping down a table, she suddenly recalled Arthur’s invitation.
She picked up her phone, about to call him, when it rang.
The caller ID showed the landline for Blackwood Estate.
“Hello?” Isabella answered.
“Good afternoon, Mrs. Blackwood. This is Charles,” the butler said with his usual respect. “Mr. Arthur Blackwood has an urgent matter requiring his immediate attention overseas. I regret to inform you that today’s meeting must be postponed.”

Isabella’s grip on the phone tightened slightly.
“I understand,” she replied, struggling to keep her voice steady. “Any idea when he might return?”
“That remains uncertain at present. I will contact you upon his return,” Charles responded.
“Alright. Thank you.”
Isabella ended the call and leaned heavily against the wall.
“What’s wrong, Mommy?” Liam asked, noticing her strained expression.
“Nothing, sweetheart.” Isabella exhaled slowly, forcing a smile. “Mommy’s going to make dinner. What would you like?”
The boy’s face brightened with excitement. “Glazed chicken!”
“Okay. Mommy will make it for you.” She ruffled his hair and moved toward the kitchen.

Isabella’s mind drifted as she cooked, only snapping back to attention at the sound of a knock.
“That must be Emily.” She dried her hands and went to answer the door.
Relief washed over her at the familiar face.
“I’m here, Miss Scott!” Emily greeted cheerfully, then her expression shifted to concern. “Are you alright? You look very pale.”
Isabella managed a weak smile.
“I’m fine, just exhausted. Liam’s watching TV. Please watch him for a bit. I need to go out.”

###Chapter 438

“Go ahead. I’ll look after the little one,” Emily said warmly as she stepped inside the apartment.

Isabella went back to the kitchen. She packed some roasted chicken and fresh salad into an insulated container.

The moment she stepped outside, a sharp autumn wind greeted her. She shivered inside her coat. She quickly hailed a taxi and gave the driver the hospital’s address.

Her thoughts were a jumbled mess the entire ride.

She didn’t even realize they had arrived until the driver spoke. Startled, she paid and got out.

Isabella walked into the hospital and made her way to Eleanor’s room.

She found the older woman sitting up in bed. Her gaze was distant, fixed on the window.

“Grandmother,” Isabella said softly as she approached.

Eleanor turned slowly. Her eyes brightened when she saw who it was.

“You came, Isabella,” she whispered, her voice frail.

“I brought you something to eat.” Isabella opened the container and set it on the bedside table.

“Oh, garden salad and honey-glazed chicken!” Eleanor smiled. “You always remember my favorites.”

“Then you should eat plenty, Grandmother.” Isabella served a portion onto a plate.

Eleanor had only taken a few bites when she paused. She looked intently at Isabella.

“How are things with Ethan?”

Isabella’s breath caught.

“It’s finished,” she replied, avoiding her grandmother’s eyes.

“Finished?” Eleanor repeated, confused. “What happened?”

Isabella took a deep breath. She explained everything that had occurred.

Eleanor was silent for a long time.

“Isabella,” she finally said, her voice thick with emotion. “You’ve endured so much, my dear.”

Isabella’s eyes reddened. Tears threatened to fall.

“I’m alright, Grandmother. As long as you recover, I can face anything.”

“My sweet girl.” Eleanor reached out with a trembling hand. She gently cupped Isabella’s cheek.

“I fear I am old and of little use to you now. The road ahead you must walk alone.”

“Please don’t say that, Grandmother.” Isabella clasped Eleanor’s hand in both of hers.

“You will get better, I promise. We will have a happy life. We will watch Liam grow up together.”

A loving softness filled Eleanor’s expression. She pulled her granddaughter into a tight embrace. Their quiet cries whispered through the sterile room.

Days later, light from crystal chandeliers poured into the grand ballroom. A tower of champagne glasses shimmered. Fine silverware reflected a brilliant glow. The air was rich with expensive perfume and the scent of fresh flowers. Everything spoke of luxury and prestige.

Sophia stood before a mirror. She wore a gown of fiery red, vivid as a rose in full bloom. She checked her reflection meticulously. “How is this dress? Does it suit my skin tone perfectly?” she asked Zoe, her tone dripping with pride.

Zoe offered a thin, practiced smile. “It’s stunning. You will undoubtedly be the center of attention tonight.”
###Chapter 439

Zoe hid a smirk behind her polite expression. Sophia was so confident, believing a red dress could win Ethan over.

"Of course," Sophia replied, completely missing Zoe's insincerity. She adjusted the diamonds on her gown, her eyes gleaming with ambition. "It's my birthday. Ethan will definitely surprise me today."

She walked to the floor-to-ceiling window, staring down at the lively crowd below.

"Sophia, do you truly believe Ethan will propose?" Zoe probed, joining her by the window.

Sophia turned, a flicker of doubt crossing her face before she forced her confident smile back. "Absolutely. He loves me. A proposal is inevitable." Zoe watched her feigned composure, sneering internally.

Sophia was celebrating too early, thinking she had everyone fooled.

"Really? But I heard Ethan has been spending a lot of time with Isabella lately," Zoe mentioned casually, observing Sophia's reaction.

Predictably, Sophia's expression darkened instantly. She bit her lip hard, fury flashing in her eyes.

"That wretched Isabella! How dare she try to steal Ethan from me!" Sophia's voice turned shrill, her fists clenching until her knuckles turned white.

"Calm down, Sophia," Zoe said, placing a hand on her shoulder.

"I won't let her win. I'll make sure she stays away from Ethan," Sophia seethed.

"And how do you plan to do that?" Zoe asked, her interest piqued.

Sophia took a deep breath, trying to regain her composure. She walked to the table, picked up a glass of champagne, and drained it in one gulp.

"I've made arrangements. Tonight, I will ensure that Ethan and I..." Sophia paused, her eyes burning with determination. "I will become pregnant with his child."

Zoe watched her with a hidden sneer. Sophia thought a child would secure her place with Ethan. How utterly naive!

"Best of luck with that," Zoe said dryly, raising her glass to clink against Sophia's, a hint of irony sparkling in her gaze.

The banquet hall filled rapidly, buzzing with the chatter and laughter of arriving guests. Sophia maintained a composed facade, greeting each guest with a practiced smile, though her eyes constantly darted toward the entrance, searching for Ethan.

Time passed slowly. With each minute, Ethan's absence chipped away at her composure.

"Why isn't he here yet?" she whispered to herself, her expression growing worried.

Zoe approached, feigning concern. "Is everything alright, Sophia? You seem tense."

"It's nothing. Just a bit tired," Sophia replied, masking her anxiety with a strained smile.

Just then, the banquet hall doors swung open. A tall figure stepped inside. Sophia's face instantly lit up with relief and anticipation.

"Ethan!" She moved gracefully toward the entrance, her heart swelling with hope.

But reality delivered a harsh blow.

The newcomer was not Ethan, but his assistant, Benjamin. Dressed impeccably, Benjamin gave a formal nod. "Happy birthday, Miss Reed." He presented a beautifully wrapped gift box. "This is from Mr. Blackwood."

Sophia's smile froze. She maintained her composure as she accepted the gift, though her fingers trembled slightly. Whispers spread among the guests. The once lively atmosphere grew tense.

Many attendees had hoped to meet Ethan, aiming to forge connections with Blackwood Enterprises. His absence left them visibly disappointed.
###Chapter 440
“I thought Sophia was Ethan’s beloved. Why isn’t he here himself?”
“Maybe it was all just rumors.”
The whispers cut through Sophia like shards of glass. Her cheeks burned with humiliation. She took a sharp breath, forcing her composure to remain intact.
She turned to Benjamin, her voice low but firm. “Where is Ethan? Why didn’t he come?”
Benjamin’s expression was carefully neutral, tinged with a hint of apology. “Mr. Blackwood had an urgent matter to attend to.”
“What urgent matter?” The words were practically bitten off, her teeth clenched.
She had poured her soul into planning this birthday banquet. She had imagined his arrival a thousand times. Instead, she was met with this public rejection. This humiliation was unbearable.
Benjamin gave a slight, regretful bow. “My apologies, Miss Reed. I am not privy to the details.”
His words were a final, stinging slap.
The murmurs around them grew louder, laced with open mockery.
“It seems Miss Reed’s importance to Mr. Blackwood was… overstated.”
“I believed they were close. Apparently not.”
“We should go. The show is over.”
One by one, the guests found excuses to leave. The grand hall, once buzzing with energy, fell into a hollow, echoing silence.
Sophia stood frozen, her face a ghostly white mask.
Zoe approached, her heels clicking decisively on the marble floor. A look of thinly veiled disdain was masked by feigned concern.
“Are you alright, Sophia?” Zoe’s voice dripped with false sympathy as she lightly touched Sophia’s shoulder. “Don’t be too upset. Mr. Blackwood must have a critical situation to handle.”
Sophia’s head snapped up, eyes blazing.
“He cleared his entire schedule for today,” she hissed.
An unreadable flicker passed over Zoe’s face. She paused, as if a thought had just occurred to her. “Oh, that’s right. Today is Parents’ Day at the kindergarten. The one Liam Scott attends.”
Confusion flashed across Sophia’s features.
Zoe’s tone was casual, yet deliberate. “Perhaps… Mr. Blackwood went there instead?”
Sophia’s breath caught in her throat.
Impossible!
She shook her head violently, trying to dislodge the terrifying thought. A wave of sickening discomfort churned in her chest. Her hands clenched into fists, nails digging deep into her palms.
“I have to see for myself,” she muttered through gritted teeth. She spun around and stormed toward the dressing room.
Zoe watched her go, a cold smirk twisting her lips.
Driven by sheer curiosity and boredom, Zoe decided to follow. She wouldn’t miss the unfolding drama.
Sophia changed into a simple outfit, grabbed her bag, and rushed out. She drove through the streets, thoughts of Ethan and Liam swirling like a storm in her mind.
She would not lose. She would not let Isabella take him from her.
The sun was brilliant. Colorful flags fluttered gaily in the breeze. The sound of cheerful children’s songs spilled from the kindergarten gates.
Ethan stepped onto the scene. His casual attire did nothing to diminish his powerful presence. His arrival immediately drew the attention of every parent. Even the young teachers couldn’t help but steal glances.

###Chapter 441

A teacher with a shy smile and a neat ponytail approached him. “Excuse me, sir. May I ask which child you’re here for?”

“Liam Scott,” Ethan replied.

The teacher, Emma Collins, paused for a moment. “Liam… Please, follow me.”

She guided Ethan toward a quieter section of the playground.

From a distance, he spotted a small figure standing rigidly opposite a much larger boy.

“Where’s your dad? Do you even have one?” the heavier boy sneered, hands planted on his hips.

“My dad is just very busy,” Liam insisted, his cheeks flushed with defiance.

“Liar! You don’t have a dad. Your mom probably—”

A woman draped in expensive fur and glittering jewels cut him off. “Your mother must have picked some random man. You’re just a fatherless child.”

Liam’s small hands balled into fists. Tears welled in his eyes, but he refused to let them fall. “That’s not true! Don’t talk about my mom like that!” He shoved the other boy with all his strength.

The boy stumbled backward and landed hard on the ground. He immediately began to wail.

“How dare you touch my son!” the fur-clad woman shrieked, lunging toward Liam.

Ms. Collins quickly stepped between them. “What is going on here? Why are you fighting?”

“Ms. Collins, he said horrible things about my mother,” Liam explained, his voice shaking.

“He pushed me first!” the other boy cried louder.

Ethan stepped forward.

The moment Liam saw him, his brave facade shattered. He ran and threw his arms around Ethan’s legs, clinging tightly.

Ethan’s heart clenched. He gently stroked Liam’s back. “What’s wrong, Liam?”

“They… they said awful things about Mommy,” Liam managed between sobs.

Ethan’s expression turned to ice. His gaze sharpened as it landed on the other boy and his mother.

“What exactly did they say?” Ethan’s voice was low, but it carried a weight that silenced the entire area.

The chubby boy whimpered and hid behind his mother’s coat.

The woman in fur froze the instant she recognized Ethan. Her haughty demeanor vanished. Her company had dealings with Blackwood Enterprises. She knew she couldn’t afford to cross him.

Just then, the boy’s father arrived. He had been ready to confront whoever hurt his son. He, too, froze at the sight of Ethan, his face paling.

“M—Mr. Blackwood…” he stammered, sweat beading on his forehead.

Ethan drew Liam closer into his arms. His cold eyes swept over the three of them. He said nothing, letting the tension thicken the air.

After a long silence, he finally spoke. “I am Liam’s father.”
###Chapter 442

His words detonated like a silent bomb.

The chubby boy’s parents visibly trembled.

They never imagined the quiet child they’d dismissed was the son of Ethan Blackwood.

“Our deepest apologies, Mr. Blackwood. We had no idea—” The father rushed forward, pulling his son from behind his wife. “Apologize to Mr. Blackwood and his son. Now!”

The terrified boy could barely lift his head. “Sorry,” he mumbled, voice shaking.

Ethan ignored their frantic pleas.

He turned away, his focus entirely on the little boy. “It’s alright, Liam. I’m here.” His voice was a low, steady anchor. He gently stroked Liam’s hair.

Liam clung to him, burying his face in Ethan’s shoulder. A wave of relief finally washed over the child.

The other parents watched Ethan leave, their faces pale with dread. They knew they’d just made a powerful enemy.

Ethan scanned the playground. Isabella was nowhere in sight.

“Liam,” he asked, his tone softening unconsciously. “Where’s your mother?”

Liam sniffled, pulling back slightly. “Mommy… she isn’t here yet.”

A faint pang echoed in Ethan’s chest.

He set Liam down and took his small hand. He led him to a quiet corner where toys and colorful play dough were set out.

“Shall we play for a bit?” Ethan suggested.

Liam nodded eagerly, immediately grabbing a lump of blue dough. He let go of Ethan’s hand and began shaping it.

Ethan sat beside him, watching the small, focused hands. His own mind, however, drifted far away.

Wasn’t Lucas supposed to be Liam’s father? That man had always presented himself as so devoted. It was clearly all a performance.

No one here seemed to know who Liam’s real father was.

Meanwhile, Isabella was stuck in gridlock.

Lunch hour traffic was a nightmare. She tapped her steering wheel, frantic not to miss Parents’ Day.

She finally screeched to a halt outside the kindergarten, jumping out and sprinting toward the playground.

Then she froze.

Ethan was there. He was sitting calmly with Liam, both absorbed in play dough.

Her breath hitched.

What was he doing here?

“Isabella.” His voice pulled her from her thoughts.

She walked over as he stood. “Why are you here?” she asked, confusion clear in her eyes.

“Just passing by. Thought I’d stop in,” he replied with a casual shrug.

Their conversation was cut short.

“Children, time to gather round!” Emma Collins clapped her hands, calling the class together.

Isabella moved past Ethan, waving to Liam. “Go on, sweetheart. Your teacher is calling.”

Liam obediently stood and toddled off to join the other children.

Isabella turned to head for the parents’ area. Ethan’s hand gently caught her wrist.

“It seems Lucas doesn’t prioritize you and Liam very much.”

She whirled around, stunned by his blunt words. “That’s not true,” she said, awkwardly pulling her hand free. “Lucas is just… busy.”

Ethan’s gaze was intense, piercing. “Have you ever considered finding a stepfather for Liam?”

###Chapter 443

Isabella’s head snapped up.

Her pulse quickened as her eyes met his.

“What are you implying? That’s not something you should ask.”

She turned away and walked briskly to the spectator area.

Ethan didn’t try to stop her.

A faint, knowing smile touched his lips.

He gave her a moment before following.

The games had already started by the time he took the seat beside her.

Isabella sat rigidly in the parents’ section.

Her gaze was locked on Liam.

She fought to keep her rising anxiety under control.

Then Ethan settled next to her.

Her tension spiked.

Today’s schedule included a family participation segment.

Isabella glanced down at the handbook in her lap.

She had read the rules repeatedly.

Public events like this had never been her strength.

She had thought about skipping it.

But she couldn’t say no to Liam.

Not when he looked at her with so much hope.

Liam ran over and grabbed her hand.

“Mom!” he exclaimed, bouncing with excitement. “Let’s play the game together!”

Just like that, her fears dissolved.

She gently ruffled his soft hair and smiled. “Of course.”

Ethan lingered nearby.

He watched the easy affection between mother and son.

An unconscious smile formed on his face.

“In this round, parents will carry their children and hop to the finish line,” Emma’s voice announced over the loudspeaker.

Isabella took a deep breath.

She crouched down so Liam could climb onto her back.

“Hold on tight, sweetie.”

“Okay!” Liam wrapped his small arms around her neck.

He pressed close, soaking in her warmth. “Ready, set, go!”

The starting whistle blew.

All the parents leaped forward with their children, determined.

Isabella wasn’t particularly athletic.

But she pushed herself for Liam.

Ethan followed closely, his watchful eyes never leaving her.

A flicker of concern showed in his expression.

“Go, Mommy! Go, Mr. Blackwood!” Liam cheered, waving his little hands.

Isabella gritted her teeth.

Sweat beaded on her forehead.

She refused to slow down.

They finally crossed the finish line.

Liam jumped up and down, thrilled. “Mommy, we won!”

Isabella was breathless, but her smile was radiant.

As the competition continued, they moved in perfect sync.

They dominated each round.

The final challenge was the “Three-Legged Race.”

Isabella and Ethan stood side by side.

Their ankles were tied together securely.

“Remember to move in rhythm,” Emma reminded the competing parents.

“Understood.”

Isabella and Ethan exchanged a look of determination.

“Ready, set, go!”

At the sound of the whistle, they moved forward cautiously.

At first, their steps were uncoordinated.

They nearly stumbled.

“Careful!” Ethan steadied her by gripping her waist.

Her heart raced.

A soft blush colored her cheeks. “Thanks.”

“Anytime,” he murmured, his voice low and intimate.
###Chapter 444

Their steps fell into a perfect rhythm, moving faster and with more assurance.

“Go, Mommy and Daddy!” Liam shouted from the edge of the field.

They pushed forward, crossing the finish line well ahead of the others.

Liam let out a happy shriek, snatching the trophy from Emma and holding it up. “Mommy, Daddy, we won!”

He then turned and sprinted toward Ethan, throwing his small arms around him. “Daddy, you’re the best!”

Isabella stopped breathing. She never imagined Liam would call Ethan “Daddy” so openly. Her first instinct was to correct him, but the pure joy on his face made her hold back.

A storm of conflicting feelings raged inside her.

Ethan held the boy tightly, his intense eyes fixed on Isabella, a hint of amusement dancing in his gaze.

Outside the kindergarten’s iron gate, Sophia gripped the cold bars, her knuckles white. Her furious stare burned into the three figures on the playground. Ethan’s powerful presence, Isabella’s gentle smile, Liam’s carefree laughter… it all felt like a knife twisting in her heart.

Zoe stood beside her, arms crossed, a smirk playing on her lips.

“Well, well. What a perfect little family. It seems Isabella knows exactly how to play her cards—using that child to tie Ethan down. And you, Sophia, the brilliant lawyer, might just lose it all,” Zoe said.

Sophia’s head whipped toward her, eyes blazing. “Be quiet! This isn’t over. Nothing is decided. Ethan… he would never leave me.”

“Is that what you really believe?” Zoe raised an eyebrow, her voice dripping with sarcasm. “Today, he publicly accepted that boy as his son. He spent the entire day with Isabella and the child, playing the part of a doting father. Anyone watching would think they’re a real family.”

She paused, letting her words sink in before twisting the knife. “And you? What does that make you? The discarded ex?”

Sophia’s face lost all color, her lips trembling. “He won’t abandon me! He promised…” Her voice broke. She couldn’t finish.

Zoe watched Sophia’s distress with deep satisfaction.

A few hours later, the kindergarten event came to an end. The lively noise faded as families began to pack up and leave.

Isabella held Liam’s small hand, walking beside Ethan. The boy was still buzzing with excitement, his cheeks flushed, clutching a handmade toy robot.

“Mommy, today was the best! Can we do it again soon?” Liam asked, skipping happily.

“Of course,” Isabella replied softly, her heart warmed by his happiness.

Her phone buzzed, interrupting the moment. She glanced at the screen and stepped away to take the call.

Liam watched her go, then turned to Ethan, his eyes shining with a serious, thoughtful light.

After a moment of quiet contemplation, he nodded to himself, as if reaching a big decision. He dug into his pocket, pulling out a crumpled piece of paper and a small pencil. Carefully, he wrote down a string of numbers and handed it to Ethan.

“This is my phone number,” Liam announced in his sweet, childish voice. “You can call me if you need anything.”

He puffed out his little chest, looking unbearably proud and adorable.

Ethan looked at the slip of paper in his hand, a wave of warmth flooding his chest. He couldn’t resist reaching out and ruffling the boy’s hair. But just then, his eyes caught a slender, familiar figure in the distance.

###Chapter 445
Sophia approached them with a fragile expression, her eyes rimmed red from recent tears.
She had just witnessed the tender moment between Ethan and Isabella, a sharp pang of envy twisting inside her. But she quickly masked it with practiced ease.
Ethan’s brow furrowed as she drew near.
“Ethan,” Sophia murmured softly. “I got the birthday present you sent through Benjamin. I adore it.”
She hesitated briefly before continuing, “Are you free tonight? I was hoping we could have dinner together—it is my birthday, after all. You never missed celebrating it when we were together…”
Her voice trembled slightly, eyes wide and hopeful.
Just then, Eleanor finished her phone call and overheard Sophia’s words. A knot of anxiety tightened in her chest.
Assuming Ethan would leave with Sophia, Eleanor quickly took Liam’s small hand. “You should go,” she told Ethan. “You’ve helped enough today. We won’t take up any more of your time.”
Ethan felt a strange heaviness in his chest. His eyes followed Eleanor as she began guiding Liam away. He opened his mouth to speak, but no sound came out.
Sophia noticed his hesitation immediately. “Eleanor,” she interjected smoothly, “why not join us for dinner?” When Eleanor didn’t respond, she added gently, “Are you still upset with me? I truly had nothing to do with what happened to your grandmother…”
Eleanor’s free hand clenched into a tight fist, nails digging deep into her palm.
She spun around to face Sophia, her voice icy and sharp. “You should be grateful my grandmother is alright. If she weren’t, I would make sure you paid for it.”
Without another word, she lifted Liam into her arms and walked away, not once looking back.
The atmosphere grew thick with tension as Ethan and Sophia stood alone. “Ethan,” Sophia whispered, her eyes glistening with unshed tears. She looked more vulnerable than ever. “Have you really forgotten everything we shared?”
Ethan finally turned to her. For a moment, her words almost swayed him—but then clarity returned.
“Sophia,” he said, his tone gentle yet distant. “I have urgent work at the office. Let’s do dinner another time.” He paused, choosing his words carefully. “Today… isn’t good.”
Sophia felt the blood drain from her face. She stared at him, stunned. “But—it’s my birthday. You always used to…” Her voice broke, unable to finish.
Ethan looked away, adjusting his cuff with sudden focus. “I need to go.”
He walked off without waiting for a reply, his steps firm and decisive. Sophia stood frozen, watching him leave. Each step felt like a blow to her heart.
She couldn’t accept this. Why did Eleanor have everything?
Eleanor had Ethan’s love and a beautiful son, while she had nothing.
Resentment, long festering, flared hot within her. Sophia gritted her teeth, her gaze turning fierce. She would ruin Eleanor, no matter what it took. Then she would claim what was rightfully hers.
Unseen in the shadows, Zoe watched everything unfold. A cold smile touched her lips as she observed Sophia’s despair. This was only the beginning. The real drama was yet to come.
She planned to watch Sophia and Eleanor destroy each other, then step in to claim the spoils.
Meanwhile, Ethan quickened his pace, determined to catch up with Eleanor and Liam.

###Chapter 446

Isabella walked quickly under the streetlights, her shadow stretching long behind her.

Liam pressed his face against her chest, his small features tight with concern.

“Isabella!” Ethan’s voice cut through the night, sharp with urgency.

She halted but refused to turn.

“We need to talk,” he stated, stopping a few steps behind her.

Isabella drew a sharp breath before finally facing him. “What more is there to say, Mr. Blackwood?” Her tone was icy.

His expression hardened. “I will find out the truth about your grandmother. As for Sophia—”

“Yes, Sophia is your first love. Your great love. So what?” Isabella exhaled, a weary sound. “I’m exhausted, Ethan. Just let me go.”

She pulled Liam closer and moved to leave, but Ethan caught her wrist.

“Isabella, listen!” His voice was a desperate plea this time.

She shook him off as tears finally escaped. “I don’t want to hear another word from you. We are done, Ethan.”

A loud, sudden growl from her stomach shattered the tense silence.

Her face flushed a deep crimson, the heat spreading to her ears.

She wished the pavement would swallow her whole.

Her own body had chosen the worst possible moment to humiliate her.

She awkwardly covered her stomach, shooting it a furious glare.

Ethan’s stern expression softened at the unexpected sound, a faint smile touching his lips.

He found her reaction strangely charming.

Liam, watching with wide eyes, quickly spoke up. “Mommy, are you hungry?” His innocent question broke the awkward spell.

Mortified, Isabella nodded, her cheeks burning hotter.

All she desired was to escape, to put immense distance between herself and Ethan.

“Then… Let’s go eat!” Liam announced brightly, his eyes sparkling.

Isabella’s instinct was to refuse, but one look at her son’s hopeful face made it impossible.

“Mommy, please? Let’s get something yummy,” he pleaded, tugging her sleeve.

With a resigned sigh, she surrendered. She could never deny him.

“Alright,” she whispered, her voice soft with defeat.

A flicker of amusement lit Ethan’s eyes.

His strategy to win over Liam first was proving correct.

“What would you like to eat?” Ethan asked, his voice a low, inviting rumble. He watched her, waiting.

Isabella kept her gaze down, avoiding his eyes. “I… don’t care,” she mumbled.

Her mind was too scattered to think about food.

Ethan’s expression shifted, a frown forming at her clear distance.

He turned to Liam. “What do you want to eat?”

“I want… hmm… I want McDonald’s!” Liam declared with eager anticipation.

“McDonald’s?” Ethan’s frown deepened. “Pick something else.” He refused instantly.
###Chapter 447

“Why?” Liam’s face fell, his disappointment clear and deep.

“Fast food isn’t a healthy option,” Ethan explained softly.

“But… I really want it…” Liam’s voice shook, tears welling in his eyes.

It felt like forever since he last had crispy chicken and golden fries. The craving was intense.

Seeing her son so close to crying, Isabella stepped in. “It’s just one meal.”

She didn’t want to upset Liam or start a disagreement with Ethan over something so small.

Ethan’s eyes met Isabella’s, and he felt his resistance fading.

“Fine. Just this once,” he finally agreed.

“Yes! Mr. Blackwood, you’re amazing!” Liam shouted, wrapping his small arms around Ethan’s leg.

Ethan stiffened at the sudden touch.

It felt… strange.

He glanced down at the little boy, and a gentle smile formed on his lips.

Watching them, Isabella felt a mix of emotions swirling inside.

She was torn between joy and worry.

She wanted Liam to connect with Ethan but feared their bond growing too strong.

“Let’s go,” Ethan said, pulling her from her thoughts.

He took Liam’s hand and walked toward the parking area.

Isabella followed quietly, her heart aching with a bittersweet feeling.

Ethan’s car, a sleek black Rolls-Royce Phantom, was parked nearby, radiating elegance and power.

He opened the door for Liam, then turned to Isabella.

“Get in,” he said, his tone neutral.

After a brief pause, Isabella slid into the car.

The drive was silent, filled with unspoken tension.

Only Liam’s cheerful chatter broke the quiet, a sharp contrast to the adults’ silence.

The KFC was bustling with noise and energy.

Liam, holding Ethan’s hand, pointed excitedly at the menu. “Mr. Blackwood, I want that! And that! And that too!”

Ethan listened patiently, a soft smile playing on his lips.

Isabella stood to the side, observing quietly.

How perfect things could be if her relationship with Ethan were simpler.

A wave of longing washed over her.

Once seated by the window, Liam dug into his meal with enthusiasm. “So good! So yummy!” he mumbled between bites.

Ethan watched the boy eat with such joy, his expression thoughtful but silent.

He took a small bite of a burger, his brow furrowing. Was this really that enjoyable? He doubted it.

Ethan couldn’t understand why Liam loved this food so much.

Seeing Ethan’s hesitant look, Isabella couldn’t help but laugh.

Ethan shot her a quick glance but said nothing.

He continued eating his burger quietly, occasionally looking out the window. Nearby, under a large tree, a figure stood silently, seemingly waiting.
﻿
###Chapter 448

The man was Benjamin Clark, Ethan's most trusted assistant.

Ethan gave a subtle signal with his eyes.

Benjamin understood immediately and nodded in confirmation.

After dinner, Ethan offered to drive Isabella and Liam home.

Isabella quickly declined. "That's really not necessary. We can manage." She was wary of getting too close to Ethan.

"It's late. I don't feel comfortable with you two traveling alone at night," Ethan stated calmly, his tone firm.

"It's fine, we..." Isabella started, but Ethan cut her off decisively.

"I'm taking you home." His voice brooked no argument.

Isabella reluctantly agreed.

They exited the restaurant and walked towards Ethan's car. He tried to start the engine, but it failed to turn over.

Ethan feigned confusion. "What's wrong with this thing?" He tried several more times, but the car remained silent.

"Is it broken?" Isabella asked, concern creeping into her voice.

"Seems that way," Ethan replied, his expression convincingly dismayed.

Isabella grew worried. "What should we do now?"

"Don't worry. It's not far to your place from here," Ethan suggested, glancing around.

Isabella checked the time. It was indeed very late.

After a brief hesitation, she agreed. "Alright, we can walk."

"Just a moment," Ethan said unexpectedly.

Isabella looked puzzled. "Is something wrong?"

Ethan's voice was laced with genuine concern. "Given the hour, it's not safe for you and Liam to walk home alone. Please, allow me to accompany you until you're safely inside." His eyes held a sincere worry.

The offer took Isabella by surprise.

This was not the Ethan she thought she knew.

A wave of emotion washed over her.

Faced with his earnest gaze, she found herself speechless.

"I'm not sure that's a good idea," Isabella said, her voice trembling slightly.

Ethan responded with confidence. "Why hesitate? As Liam's... friend, it's my duty to ensure you both get home safely." His expression was one of absolute certainty.

Isabella opened her mouth to protest again, but then stopped herself.

"Okay, fine," she conceded reluctantly.

She took Liam's hand, and together with Ethan, they began walking home. The streetlights stretched their shadows long on the pavement.

Liam walked between them, one hand in Isabella's, the other in Ethan's, a look of pure happiness on his face.

A faint smile touched Ethan's lips, his eyes reflecting a deep sense of contentment.

Suddenly, Liam stopped. "Mommy, I'm too tired to walk."

"I'll carry you," Isabella said, immediately bending down.

"No, let me," Ethan interjected, effortlessly scooping Liam into his arms.

"Thank you, Mr. Blackwood," Liam said sweetly.

"You're welcome," Ethan replied softly, his voice gentle. He continued walking, carrying Liam securely.

###Chapter 449

They finally arrived at Isabella’s apartment building.

“Alright, you can go up now. I’ll head out,” Ethan said.

“Okay,” Isabella replied, turning toward the entrance.

“Wait, Isabella,” he called out.

She paused and looked back. “Is there something else?”

Ethan shifted slightly, a hint of awkwardness in his posture. “I’m a bit thirsty. Could I come up for a glass of water?”

The request took her by surprise.

She hadn’t expected this from him.

Was he serious?

Her expression tightened.

“You should go home, Mr. Blackwood. It’s late,” she said coolly.

Seeing her distant demeanor, Ethan felt a pang of disappointment.

But he didn’t back down.

“After walking you all the way home, don’t I at least deserve a glass of water? Is that too much to ask?” His tone held a faint note of reproach.

Faced with his almost pitiful look, Isabella felt her irritation flare.

She held back a sigh. “Mr. Blackwood, what are you really trying to do?”

“Just a glass of water, like I said,” he replied, his expression utterly sincere.

Looking into his earnest eyes, Isabella felt her resistance waver.

“Fine. Come up,” she relented, leading him to the elevator and pressing the button.

Behind her, a subtle smile touched Ethan’s lips as he followed closely.

Inside the elevator, the silence between them grew heavy and tense.

Outside, Benjamin shivered as a cold gust swept through.

He glanced up toward Isabella’s apartment window, his mind swirling with confusion.

“Sabotaging his own car just to spend more time with his wife? What time is it even? Mr. Blackwood is really going all out. And I’ve gone to great lengths for his sake too. It’s freezing. Why hasn’t he come back down yet? What’s taking so long?”

The wind picked up, and Benjamin pulled his coat tighter, grumbling under his breath.

His eyes remained fixed on the elevator doors, waiting impatiently for Ethan’s return.

Being the CEO’s special assistant was no easy job.

The elevator doors slid open with a soft ding. Isabella stepped out first, followed by Ethan carrying Liam.

As soon as they entered the apartment, Liam squirmed free and dashed into the living room. “Mr. Blackwood, look! That’s my block castle!”

Ethan followed the direction of the little boy’s chubby finger. In the corner stood an elaborate castle built from colorful blocks, nearly as tall as an adult.

“Wow! That’s incredible!” Ethan’s praise was genuine, filled with just the right amount of awe. “Did you build that all by yourself?”

“Uh-huh!” Liam nodded, his eyes shining with pride. “And over there! Look, Mr. Blackwood! Look at my drawings!”

He tugged Ethan toward the wall where several vibrant drawings were displayed. Ethan knelt to examine them. The wall was adorned with childlike sketches of flowers, trees, imaginative cosmic scenes, and one particular drawing of a family of three holding hands in a park.

###Chapter 450

Ethan’s chest tightened painfully.

He pointed to the last drawing. “Is this you, your mom, and your dad?”

Liam nodded at first, then shook his head. “It’s my wish. I wish my mommy and daddy and I could always be together.”

A shadow fell over the boy’s bright expression.

Ethan felt the words like a physical blow. He glanced at Isabella, who stood to the side with a tense expression. She immediately averted her gaze, clearly avoiding him.

He turned his attention back to Liam. “Your wish will definitely come true,” he said, forcing a smile.

“Really?” Liam’s face instantly brightened.

“Of course. As long as you’re a good boy and do well in school, your mommy and daddy will love you very much.”

Isabella stiffened at his words. She couldn’t stop herself from calling out in a warning tone, “Ethan—”

“Liam.” He smoothly cut her off. “Can I play building blocks with you?”

“Yes!” The little boy clapped his hands in delight and pulled Ethan toward his half-built castle.

Isabella’s emotions churned as she watched them. A part of her knew this was just an act for Ethan. Yet another part of her, a part she hated, desperately longed for moments like this. She wished time would freeze, allowing them to stay like this forever.

Ethan used the opportunity to study the entire apartment. It was a modest two-bedroom unit. The furniture was old, the sofa showed signs of wear, and a desk in the corner was piled high with design sketches and various professional books. Every corner of this space was a testament to the struggles Isabella had faced. The realization sent another sharp pang through his heart. He couldn’t begin to imagine how she had managed all these years—raising a child completely alone while simultaneously building her career from nothing.

“Liam, will you play by yourself for a little while? I need to talk to your mom.” Ethan excused himself and walked over to Isabella. She stood by the window, staring out at the city lights, seemingly lost in thought.

“What are you thinking about?” he asked softly, moving to stand beside her. His voice pulled her abruptly back to the present.

“Nothing,” she muttered, throwing a brief, guarded glance his way.

“Are you still angry with me?” Ethan asked, his voice gentle.

Isabella remained silent.

He knew then that she was still holding a grudge over recent events. He sighed. “I know you’re still upset. But I had my reasons three years ago.”

“Reasons?” Isabella scoffed. “So you’re saying you were justified in teaming up with Sophia to deceive me and destroy my family?”

“That’s not it—I never intended to hurt you or your family.”

“Then tell me, what really happened back then?” Her voice rose, sharp with emotion. “What made you investigate the car accident? And why did you hide it from me?”

Ethan hesitated, biting back the words that were on the tip of his tongue.

“Well?” she demanded sharply. “Why aren’t you saying anything? You don’t actually have a reasonable explanation, do you? You were just lying to me.”

“Isabella, please trust me. I’m not lying.” His eyes were filled with a sincerity that felt real. “It’s just that… there are certain things I can’t tell you right now.”

“Is that so?” she asked, her tone dripping with sarcasm. She almost laughed in his face. “What do you take me for, Ethan? A child?”

“Enough! I don’t want to hear any more of your lies. I already told you—it’s over between us. Don’t show up in my life again.”
﻿
###Chapter 451

“Isabella, I know you despise me, but—”

“I told you to stop speaking!” Isabella’s breathing turned sharp with fury. “I refuse to waste another second on you. Get out. You are not welcome in my home.”

“What’s happening, Mommy?” Hearing the raised voices, Liam hurried in from the living area. He gazed up at the adults, his small face creased with worry.

“It’s nothing, darling.” Isabella’s tone softened as she forced a tight smile. “Mommy was just having a discussion with Mr. Blackwood.”

“Okay,” Liam murmured, nodding slowly though he understood little.

Ethan swallowed the heavy emotion stuck in his throat.

He knew further argument was pointless.

“Fine. I’ll go.” His eyes held hers, brimming with unspoken words. “But mark my words—I am not giving up on you.”

He turned sharply and walked away.

Isabella stood motionless, watching his figure vanish. Tears pooled in her eyes, spilling over in silent streams.

She sank to the floor, pulling Liam close as sobs shook her frame.

“Mommy, what’s wrong?” His small hands patted her back gently. “Please don’t be sad…”

“It’s alright, sweetie…” She tried to smile, but the tears wouldn’t stop.

Suddenly, noise erupted from outside.

“Isabella Scott, come out here!”

“You home-wrecking witch! You stole my man, and you’ll pay for it!” The night air cracked with venomous shouts.

Isabella wiped her face, expression hardening. She knew that voice. It was Sophia, screaming curses.

“Stay right here, baby,” she whispered to Liam before standing and marching toward the door.

Down below, Sophia stood with a small mob, hostility rolling off them.

Isabella’s eyes narrowed. “What do you want, Sophia?”

Sophia sneered, crossing her arms. “What do I want? How dare you ask me that? You shameless snake. How could you seduce Ethan? You need to be taught a lesson.”

Isabella let out a cold laugh. “Seduce him? Have you lost your mind? Whatever was between Ethan and me ended long ago. Stop inventing drama.”

“Ended?” Sophia’s face twisted with rage. “You expect me to believe that? You can’t stand to see me happy. You want to take everything from me!”

Isabella’s jaw clenched. “Take from you? Sophia, don’t play the victim. You were the one who took everything from me. You almost cost me my grandmother!”

“I did no such thing!” Sophia shot back, voice rising. “Whatever happened, you brought it upon yourself!”

Isabella exhaled sharply. “You know the truth. You just refuse to admit it.” She stepped forward, tone firm. “This is your last warning, Sophia. Leave now, or face the consequences.”

Sophia laughed mockingly. “Oh? And what will you do? You think I’m afraid of you? Today, I’ll show you what happens when you cross me.”

Her eyes flicked to the group behind her. “Teach her a lesson!” At her command, the crowd surged toward Isabella.

But before they could reach her, a tall figure stepped in front, shielding her completely.

###Chapter 452

“Don’t you dare touch her.”

Ethan’s voice cut through the air like ice. Sharp. Commanding.

His eyes locked onto the men surrounding Isabella. They froze under his glare. The aggression in their posture vanished instantly.

Sophia’s face went pale.

“Ethan?” Her voice shook. “What are you doing here?”

“If I hadn’t arrived, what were you planning to do?” His tone was cold. Empty.

She opened her mouth. No sound came out.

His voice dropped lower. “This is your final warning. Stay away from Isabella. Next time, there will be consequences.”

Sophia trembled. She knew he meant it. Clenching her jaw, she turned sharply.

“We’re leaving.” She stormed off, her followers scrambling behind her.

Once they were gone, Ethan turned to Isabella. He took in her pale face. Something painful flickered in his gaze.

“Are you hurt?” he asked softly.

She shook her head. “I’m okay.”

He hesitated. “I should have been here sooner.”

“It’s not your fault.” She drew a slow breath, steadying herself. “But thank you.”

His expression softened. “Since when do we thank each other?”

She looked away. A simple thank you couldn’t fix what was broken between them. She needed to leave. To put space between them before she got lost in him again.

But his presence held her. An invisible force. His gaze was unwavering. She couldn’t move.

She knew the danger. Sophia’s madness. Victoria’s cruelty. And most of all—Ethan’s uncertain heart.

Isabella stepped into the elevator. Her mind raced. She gripped the railing until her knuckles turned white.

Stay calm. Think logically. Don’t fall for him again.

She reached her apartment and locked the door quickly. Shutting herself inside. Alone.

Outside, Ethan stood motionless. His eyes fixed on where she had disappeared. He didn’t move. Lost in thought.

A heavy sigh escaped him. Exhaustion. Regret.

There was no path back for them. The wounds from three years ago ran too deep. No matter what he did, the pain he caused still remained.

He pulled out his phone and called Benjamin.

“Assign a team to watch Isabella. Keep her safe.”

“Understood, Mr. Blackwood,” Benjamin replied.

Even after hanging up, Ethan’s mood didn’t lift. Memories haunted him. Warnings from the past.

“You’ll regret this, Ethan. You’ll lose her forever.”

He had laughed then. Now, he knew it was true. The regret was crushing.

If he hadn’t misjudged her three years ago. If he hadn’t pushed her away. Liam could have been his son. They could have been a family.

###Chapter 453
Life doesn't grant second chances.
His moment had vanished, lost to the relentless march of time.
A bitter smile touched Ethan's lips as he turned to leave.
A figure suddenly blocked his path.
Under the harsh streetlight stood Sophia, her face pale and etched with misery.
“What are you doing here?” Ethan's voice was icy, devoid of warmth.
“I needed to explain. I never intended…” Sophia's words trembled, tears welling in her eyes.
He cut her off sharply. “Stop. I have no interest in your excuses.” He attempted to step around her, but she clung to his arm.
“Please, Ethan. Just listen…”
He jerked his arm away violently. Sophia stumbled, falling hard onto the cold pavement.
A sharp cry escaped her. Her hand scraped against the rough ground, blood welling from the cut.
“How could you?” She looked up, tears tracing paths through her makeup, her voice a raw mix of hurt and panic. Ethan stared down, his expression unyielding.
His cold gaze made her shiver. Her face lost all remaining color.
“Ethan, I admit it. I was wrong to treat Isabella that way. My love for you… it made me afraid. I thought I would lose you…” Her voice faded to a whisper.
“Love?” He laughed, a harsh, dismissive sound.
“Ethan…” she tried again, but he interrupted. “Let's be clear, Sophia. The only reason you're still here is because you once saved my life. Without that, you'd have been gone long ago.”
His words felt like a physical blow.
Her lips trembled. No sound came out. She couldn't let this be the end. There had to be a way.
“Ethan… I'm begging you. Please.” Her voice broke. “I'll disappear. You'll never see me again.” A flicker of surprise crossed his features.
“I mean it. I swear I'll go,” she insisted, her tone desperate.
“You must guarantee you will never approach me or Isabella again,” he demanded coldly.
“I give you my word. I swear it,” she vowed, her voice shaking with intensity.
She paused, watching him cautiously. “There's one more thing…” She hesitated. “The International Jewelry Design Competition… Could you… Could you ensure my friend Zoe wins?”
The request surprised him. Unaware of Isabella's participation, he shrugged. “Do what you want. It's a meaningless contest.”
The outcome mattered little compared to ensuring Isabella's peace.
“Thank you, Ethan. Thank you…” Relief washed over her face as she repeated her gratitude.
Without another word, Ethan turned and walked away. He didn't look back. Sophia watched him leave, her expression slowly twisting into a vicious smirk.
“So, Isabella, you think you've won?” she muttered to the empty street. “If I can't have him, neither will you.”

###Chapter 454

Isabella stood up.
She wiped her tears away.
Her clothes were straightened.
She took out her phone.
Then she called Zoe.

“It’s done. Ethan agreed. You will win the competition…”

Ethan slumped into his car seat.
He closed his eyes.
Fatigue washed over him.

At the grand Grant Manor.
The massive redwood doors stood firmly shut.
Intricate carvings covered them.
They radiated timeless sophistication.

In the living room.
Lucas stood tense.
His car keys gripped tightly.
Urgency marked his expression.

“Mom, I have to go. There's a mountain of work at the office,” he said impatiently.

Eleanor sat across the room.
Elegant sofa, dark red outfit.
Her hair was neatly swept up.
A jade bracelet adorned her wrist.
Symbols of her refined life.
But today, concern replaced her poise.
She sighed gently.
Weariness and exasperation tinged her voice.

“Lucas, why must you be so stubborn? Do you think I'd lie to you? I felt a tightness in my chest this morning. That's why I asked you to come.”

“Mom!” Lucas’s voice rose slightly. “If you're truly unwell, you should be at the hospital. Not calling me back here.”

Eleanor’s expression darkened.
She placed her water glass down.
A soft ‘clink’ sounded.
The water surface rippled quietly.

“I didn't want to worry you unnecessarily. Just having you here comforts me,” she replied softly.

Lucas studied his mother.
He saw through her motives clearly.
She wasn't sick.
This was a pretense.

“Mom, let's be direct. You called me here because of Isabella, didn't you?” Lucas stated bluntly.

Eleanor’s face stiffened momentarily.
Then composure returned.
Her ruse was exposed.
She dropped the act.

“Lucas, I know you have feelings for Isabella. But… she has a child. Our family’s reputation could suffer if this gets out.”

“Mom!” Lucas cut her off, irritated. “What is wrong with Isabella? She's independent. She earns her living honestly. She deserves no scorn. You have no right to judge her.”

“I'm not judging her,” Eleanor attempted to justify.
But Lucas’s protective expression was immovable.

“Mom, I'm telling you for the last time. Stay out of my personal life,” Lucas said firmly.
